Title: Sulfate is transported at significant rates through the symbiosome membrane and is crucial for nitrogenase biosynthesis

Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Essential sulfur source for cysteine, methionine, glutathione and especially nitrogenase biosynthesis during symbiotic nitrogen fixation
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes sulfate transport from host plant cytosol across the symbiosome membrane into bacteroids, enabling high bacteroid sulfur accumulation and nitrogenase biosynthesis; loss of SST1 reduces sulfate delivery and symbiotic nitrogen fixation
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. NanoSIMS showed bacteroids accumulated about 20-fold more sulfur than host nodule cytosol in wild type. 2. In sst1-1 nodules, 34S enrichment in bacteroids was strongly reduced, confirming SST1 is required for efficient sulfate transport across the symbiosome membrane. 3. Loss of SST1 inhibited de novo nitrogenase biosynthesis, reduced nifH transcript and nitrogenase protein abundance, and caused defective nodule function/senescence under symbiotic conditions.
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: sst1-1 mutant analysis; 34S-sulfate stable isotope labeling; NanoSIMS imaging; LC-MS/MS isotope incorporation into proteins; qRT-PCR; label-free proteomics
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: High in nodules/symbiosome membrane as described for SST1 protein accumulation
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Symbiotic nodulation with Mesorhizobium loti R7A; 34S-sulfate feeding for 96 h; sulfur deficiency caused by sst1 mutation
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Residual 34S accumulation in mutant bacteroids suggests some uncontrolled diffusion across the symbiosome membrane, but active transport via SST1 is required to reach wild-type sulfate levels. The mutation affects growth under symbiotic but not nonsymbiotic conditions.
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Loss of function causes inefficient N2 fixation, reduced nodule performance, early nodule senescence, lower leghemoglobin and nitrogenase levels, bacteroid lysis, and reduced plant growth under symbiotic conditions
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: A strong target to improve sulfur use efficiency and maintain nitrogen fixation in legumes on sulfur-deficient soils; should be preserved or enhanced in breeding for robust symbiotic performance
Common_Genes.Omics_Data: Proteomics (LC-MS/MS, MaxQuant); NanoSIMS isotope imaging; qRT-PCR
